key_drivers: US-Israel strikes on Iran triggering geopolitical risk-off; VIX spiked to 23.76 (+12.4%), MOVE index surged to 74.53 (+6.4%)
10Y Treasury yield rising to 4.145% (4th consecutive session); real yields trending higher, compressing equity multiples and pressuring gold
DXY at 99.06 (+0.29% day, +1.34% week) as rate cut bets pared back; Fed holds neutral, investors pricing <50% odds of 2+ cuts in 2026
WTI crude above $80, Brent ~$84-85 on Strait of Hormuz supply disruption fears; oil supply shock dominates energy bias
Gold ETF inflows $5.3B in Feb + $1.2B GLD surge early March; but rising real yields and strong DXY create headwinds for metals
RBA hawkish (hiked to 3.85%, March hike possible); but China official PMI contracted to 49.0 and risk-off environment weigh on AUD
BoJ on hold at 0.75% (March hike odds ~5%); JPY benefits from safe-haven demand and eventual rate differential narrowing

fallbacks_used: Real yields: TradingView/Medium article showing 10Y TIPS ~1.77%, rising trend confirmed by 4-session Treasury yield rise
HY OAS: News-based assessment (Seeking Alpha, DailyDAC) showing spreads starting to widen above 40-week EMA
2s10s: Reuters/Barrons data showing 2Y=3.554%, 10Y=4.145%, spread ~54-59bps, flattening trend confirmed
Gold ETF flows: World Gold Council news release (Feb 2026 data: $5.3B inflows; March early data: $1.2B GLD surge)
